name: Opus Genetics, Inc.
business: Opus Genetics, Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing gene therapies to restore vision and prevent blindness in patients with inherited retinal diseases (IRDs) and other therapies for additional ophthalmic disorders. Its pipeline includes seven AAV-based gene therapy programs and Phentolamine Ophthalmic Solution 0.75%, an FDA-approved small-molecule therapy for pharmacologically induced mydriasis. The company also has a license and collaboration agreement with Viatris for the development and commercialization of PS.
sellsTo: Patients with night vision disturbances after keratorefractive surgery

exposures.sentence: Our pipeline includes seven adeno-associated virus (AAV)-based programs, led by OPGx-LCA5 for LCA5-related mutations and OPGx-BEST1 for BEST1-related retinal degeneration, with additional candidates targeting RHO, CNGB1, RDH12, NMNAT1, and MERTK.

Our pipeline also includes Phentolamine Ophthalmic Solution 0.75%, a small-molecule therapy that is FDA-approved for pharmacologically induced mydriasis, with additional potential indications in presbyopia and low-light visual disturbances following keratorefractive surgery.
APX3330 is a selective small molecule that is designed to act on the dual-functioning Apurinic/Apyrimidinic Endonuclease 1/Redox Effector Factor-1 (APE1/Ref-1) protein, referred to as Ref-1.

catalysts.event: Dosing with OPGx-LCA5 in Phase 3 portion anticipated
catalysts.sentence: Following availability of validated clinical drug supply manufactured with the intended commercial processes, dosing with OPGx-LCA5 is anticipated in the second half of 2026 with topline clinical data expected approximately one year later.

catalysts.event: Topline data from OPGx-LCA5 Phase 3 expected
catalysts.sentence: Following availability of validated clinical drug supply manufactured with the intended commercial processes, dosing with OPGx-LCA5 is anticipated in the second half of 2026 with topline clinical data expected approximately one year later.

catalysts.event: PDUFA action date for PS presbyopia sNDA
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, the FDA accepted the sNDA and set a Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA) action date of October 17, 2026.

catalysts.event: Topline results from LYNX-3 trial expected
catalysts.sentence: Topline results from LYNX-3 are expected in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Presentation of Phase 1/2 six-month pediatric cohort data at ARVO
catalysts.sentence: We are planning to present the Phase 1/2 six-month pediatric cohort data at the annual meeting of the Association for Research in Vision and Ophthalmology (ARVO) conference in May 2026.

catalysts.event: Three-month results from OPGx-BEST1 Cohort 1 expected
catalysts.sentence: To date, two participants have been treated in the study, representing both dominant and recessive forms of BEST disease, with three-month results from the entire Cohort 1 expected mid-year 2026.

tamClaims.claim: LCA5 affects approximately 170 people in the U.S. and 3,260 people worldwide.
tamClaims.sentence: LCA5 is a severe, early-onset form of IRD that accounts for roughly 2% of all LCA cases, or approximately 170 people in the U.S. and 3,260 people worldwide.

tamClaims.claim: BEST1 affects an estimated 8,400 people in the U.S. and 22,050 people worldwide.
tamClaims.sentence: BEST1 accounts for ~3.5% of all IRDs and affects an estimated 8,400 people in the U.S. and 22,050 people worldwide.

tamClaims.claim: RHO-adRP affects approximately 8,800 people in the U.S., and 30,580 people worldwide.
tamClaims.sentence: RHO-adRP affects approximately 8,800 people in the U.S., and 30,580 people worldwide, making it one of the most common IRDs.

tamClaims.claim: Presbyopia affects an estimated 128 million Americans, and approximately 2 billion people worldwide.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that 128 million Americans, and approximately 2 billion people worldwide, have presbyopia, and this number is expected to grow as the population ages.

tamClaims.claim: There are approximately 100 million eye dilations in the United States performed annually.
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 100 million eye dilations in the United States performed annually.

tamClaims.claim: There are 600,000 to 700,000 laser vision correction procedures conducted every year in the U.S., of these, 35% of LASIK patients report dim light disturbances post treatment.
tamClaims.sentence: There are 600,000 to 700,000 laser vision correction procedures conducted every year in the U.S., of these, 35% of LASIK patients report dim light disturbances post treatment.
